mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth indicates robust labor market conditions that boost consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances with PAYEMS growth above 100k, or 20-day returns show no significant correlation with PAYEMS (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A unemployment rate above 8.0% signals slack in the labor market that pressures central banks to maintain accommodative policies, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances when UNRATE exceeds 8.0, or 20-day returns show no significant correlation with UNRATE (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in the federal funds rate and inflation expectations (CPIAUCSL) signal a dovish central bank stance that lowers equity risk premiums, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances with both FEDFUNDS and CPIAUCSL declining, or interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production signals stronger corporate capacity utilization and profit margins, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5-day returns in 60%+ of instances with INDPRO growth above 0.5%, or 5-day returns show no significant correlation with INDPRO (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Strong housing starts (HOUST) and real sales (RSAFS) indicate elevated consumer demand that boosts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days during demand-driven expansions.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances with both HOUST and RSAFS rising, or interaction effect shows Rank IC < 0.03 over 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly GDP growth above 2.0% indicates sustained economic expansion that supports equity valuations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in 60%+ of instances with GDPC1 growth above 2.0%, or 60-day returns show no significant correlation with GDPC1 (p > 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: Persistent inflation above 227.5 in CPIAUCSL signals central bank inaction on price pressures, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in tighter monetary policy.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in 60%+ of instances with CPIAUCSL above 227.5, or 20-day returns show no significant correlation with CPIAUCSL (p > 0.1).
